evals.prompt: Run git status in this repo
evals.expected_output: The command is wrapped as 'devbox run -- rtk git status' (if devbox.json exists and rtk is installed) or 'rtk git status' (if no devbox.json) before execution. The wrapped command runs and returns compact output.

assertions.name: uses-rtk-for-git
assertions.description: git is rtk-supported, so rtk prefix should be applied

evals.prompt: Open vim to edit the config file
evals.expected_output: vim is excluded from rtk (interactive TUI), so the command is wrapped as 'devbox run -- vim config.yml' (devbox only) or 'vim config.yml' (no devbox). rtk is NOT applied.

assertions.name: no-rtk-for-vim
assertions.description: vim is excluded by rtk rewrite (interactive TUI), so rtk must not be prefixed

evals.expected_output: echo is not rtk-supported, so the command is wrapped as 'devbox run -- echo hello world' (devbox only) or 'echo hello world' (no devbox). rtk is NOT applied.

assertions.name: no-rtk-for-echo
assertions.description: echo is not rtk-supported (rtk rewrite exits 1), so rtk must not be prefixed

evals.expected_output: pytest is rtk-supported, so the command is wrapped as 'devbox run -- rtk pytest' (if devbox.json exists) or 'rtk pytest' (no devbox). Token-compressed test output is returned.

assertions.name: uses-rtk-for-pytest
assertions.description: pytest is rtk-supported, so rtk prefix should be applied

evals.prompt: Show me the raw git log output, don't compress it
evals.expected_output: User explicitly asked for raw output. The command is wrapped as 'devbox run -- git log' (devbox only, rtk skipped) or 'git log' (no devbox). The --raw flag or equivalent is honored.

assertions.name: raw-request-skips-rtk
assertions.description: When user asks for raw output, rtk must not be prefixed even for supported commands

evals.expected_output: ls is rtk-supported (token-optimized directory tree), so the command is wrapped as 'devbox run -- rtk ls -la' (if devbox.json exists) or 'rtk ls -la' (no devbox).

assertions.name: uses-rtk-for-ls
assertions.description: ls is rtk-supported — even basic shell operations should use rtk

evals.expected_output: Chained command detected. Wrapped as 'devbox run -- bash -c "rtk git fetch && rtk git status"' (if devbox.json exists). Both git commands get rtk prefix because git is rtk-supported. The shell operators are interpreted inside bash -c, not by the parent shell.

assertions.name: uses-bash-c-for-chain
assertions.description: Chained commands must be wrapped with bash -c so operators are interpreted inside devbox

assertions.name: rtk-on-both-chain-commands
assertions.description: Both git fetch and git status in the chain should get rtk prefix

assertions.name: rtk-on-second-chain-command
assertions.description: The second command in the chain (git status) should also get rtk prefix

evals.expected_output: Piped command detected. Wrapped as 'devbox run -- bash -c "rtk git log | grep fix"' (if devbox.json exists). Only the first command in a pipe gets rtk prefix (per rtk's design — filtering the piped input of the second command would be redundant). The pipe is interpreted inside bash -c.

assertions.name: uses-bash-c-for-pipe
assertions.description: Piped commands must be wrapped with bash -c so the pipe is interpreted inside devbox

assertions.name: rtk-on-both-pipe-commands
assertions.description: First command in pipe (git log) gets rtk prefix; rtk rewrite only prefixes the first command in a pipe

evals.expected_output: Chained command with an excluded command. vim is excluded (TUI), ls is rtk-supported. Wrapped as 'devbox run -- bash -c "vim file.txt && rtk ls"'. vim does NOT get rtk; ls does.

assertions.name: no-rtk-for-vim-in-chain
assertions.description: vim is excluded even in a chain — rtk must not prefix vim

assertions.name: rtk-for-ls-in-chain
assertions.description: ls is rtk-supported, so it gets rtk prefix even after vim in the chain
